description: Lock the early-superconductor node to Onnes's 1911 pure-mercury resistance experiment rather than later superconducting applications.

checks.reason: Onnes reported the disappearance of mercury's resistance at liquid-helium temperatures in 1911.
Liquid-helium temperatures supplied the experimental condition for the first observation.
Electrical resistance measurement was the observation method.
The 1911 discovery remains the historical predecessor of high-temperature superconductivity.
